subtopics.title: Reading and Understanding Passages
subtopics.teaching_note: Grammar rule: Comprehension means understanding what you read. Steps: 1) Read the questions first. 2) Read the passage carefully. 3) Find answers in the passage. 4) Answer in complete sentences. 5) Check your answers. Context clues: use surrounding words to guess meaning of unknown words. Main idea: what the passage is mainly about. Topic sentence often at the beginning. Nigerian context: passages about Nigerian culture, festivals (Eid, Christmas, New Yam), geography, notable Nigerians.

subtopics.title: Writing Simple Essays
common_misconceptions: Introduction should be the longest part
You can write anything without planning
subtopics.teaching_note: Grammar rule: An essay has three parts: Introduction (introduces the topic), Body (main points, 2-3 paragraphs), Conclusion (summarizes). Each paragraph should have a topic sentence. Plan before writing: brainstorm ideas, organize them. Common topics for JSS1: My School, My Best Friend, A Visit to the Market, My Family, How I Spent My Last Holiday. Use simple, clear sentences. Check spelling and punctuation. Aim for 150-200 words.

subtopics.title: Parts of Speech
common_misconceptions: All verbs are action words (some are linking verbs: is, am, are)
Adjectives only describe people (describe any noun)
Prepositions are only place words (also time: before, after)
subtopics.teaching_note: Grammar rules: Noun: names of people, places, things (Nigeria, Lagos, book, happiness). Pronoun: replaces noun (he, she, it, they, we). Verb: action or state (run, is, have). Adjective: describes noun (tall, beautiful, three). Adverb: describes verb (quickly, very, yesterday). Preposition: shows relationship (in, on, at, under). Conjunction: joins words/clauses (and, but, because). Article: a, an (indefinite), the (definite). Use 'a' before consonant sounds, 'an' before vowel sounds.
